topPatterns.title: Review peak-hour staffing levels against last quarter's rota
topPatterns.tailoredBody: Three Reviews this week called out long waits on the Friday lunch shift. Compare last quarter's rota for that slot against what is on the floor now — most wait-time clusters trace back to a single under-staffed shift pattern.

evidence.redactedQuote: Waited 25 minutes for a sandwich at lunch on Friday.
Long queue at the till around midday — fewer staff than usual.

topPatterns.title: Run a service-recovery outreach to the week's unhappy Reviewers
topPatterns.tailoredBody: You have two 1-2 star Reviews this week from regulars who say they felt ignored. A short personal apology in the next 24 hours typically halves the chance of a follow-up complaint reaching social media.

evidence.redactedQuote: [REVIEWER] was ignored at the counter for ten minutes.

topPatterns.title: Audit the batch / supplier behind the week's quality complaints
topPatterns.tailoredBody: One product-quality complaint landed Tuesday — cross-reference your delivery logs around that date. A single bad batch is the most common explanation for an isolated quality drop.

evidence.redactedQuote: The pastries were stale on Tuesday morning.

results.capturedUserMessage:
Business: Acme Cafe
Business industry: cafe

Theme aggregates (current week vs previous week):
  - product_quality: current=1, previous=2
  - service: current=3, previous=1
  - wait_time: current=4, previous=1

Candidate Patterns (you must pick 3 from this list):
  - id="service-recovery-outreach" kind=remediation themes=[service] verticals=universal
    title: Run a service-recovery outreach to the week's unhappy Reviewers
    body: Identify the Reviewers who left 1–2 star Reviews mentioning service breakdowns this week and draft a personal apology + remediation offer. A 24-hour response window typically halves the chance of a follow-up complaint reaching social media.
    signals: Multiple service-themed 1–2 star Reviews in the same week.
  - id="service-script-refresh" kind=remediation themes=[service] verticals=universal
    title: Refresh front-line scripts for the top recurring complaint
    body: Pull the single most repeated service complaint from this week's Reviews and write a 3-line response script the team can use when a customer raises it. Practise it at the next shift huddle.
    signals: Same service complaint repeats across 3+ Reviews.
  - id="service-shadow-shift" kind=remediation themes=[service] verticals=universal
    title: Shadow a full shift to confirm the gap is real
    body: Spend one peak shift observing the customer journey end-to-end without intervening. Most repeating service complaints surface within 30 minutes of watching the live flow.
    signals: Vague service complaints that don't point at one root cause.
  - id="service-feedback-loop" kind=remediation themes=[service,other] verticals=universal
    title: Close the loop with the Reviewer once the fix lands
    body: When a service issue raised in a Review is fixed, message the Reviewer with a one-line update. Showing you read and acted on the Review is the fastest path to a revised rating.
    signals: Reviewer mentions a specific fixable issue (not a one-off mood complaint).
  - id="product-quality-batch-audit" kind=remediation themes=[product_quality] verticals=universal
    title: Audit the batch / supplier behind the week's quality complaints
    body: Cross-reference the dates of the negative product Reviews against your delivery / production logs. A single bad batch or supplier change is the most common explanation for a quality cluster.
    signals: Quality complaints cluster within a 2–3 day window.
  - id="product-quality-spec-check" kind=remediation themes=[product_quality] verticals=universal
    title: Re-check the spec on the most-complained-about item
    body: Pull the recipe / spec sheet for the item Reviewers named most often this week and confirm it still matches what the team is actually producing. Specs drift quietly over months.
    signals: One specific product / item named in multiple Reviews.
  - id="product-quality-supplier-conversation" kind=remediation themes=[product_quality] verticals=universal
    title: Open a supplier conversation with the week's complaints in hand
    body: Send the relevant supplier a concise note quoting (anonymised) Reviewer complaints. Suppliers respond faster to specific customer feedback than to generic quality concerns.
    signals: Quality complaints point at an ingredient / component you don't make in-house.
  - id="product-quality-secret-shopper" kind=remediation themes=[product_quality,service] verticals=universal
    title: Run a secret-shopper pass on the flagged item
    body: Have someone unfamiliar to the team order or use the item and report back with photos. Often catches the quality drift the regular team has stopped noticing.
    signals: Repeated complaints about an item the team insists is fine.
  - id="wait-time-staffing-review" kind=remediation themes=[wait_time] verticals=universal
    title: Review peak-hour staffing levels against last quarter's rota
    body: Compare the rota for the days Reviewers complained about waiting with the same days last quarter. Most wait-time clusters trace back to a single under-staffed shift pattern.
    signals: wait_time mentioned 3+ times in a single week.
  - id="wait-time-queue-comms" kind=remediation themes=[wait_time] verticals=universal
    title: Set an explicit wait-time expectation at the door
    body: Reviewers tolerate waits twice as long when the expected duration is stated up front. Brief the host / greeter to quote a current wait and offer a wait-list SMS.
    signals: Reviewer says the wait was unexpected, not that it was long.
  - id="wait-time-throughput-bottleneck" kind=remediation themes=[wait_time] verticals=universal
    title: Time the bottleneck step end-to-end on a busy shift
    body: Pick the step Reviewers complain about (order-taking, prep, payment) and stopwatch it across 20 customers on a busy shift. Throughput problems almost always live in one step, not the whole flow.
    signals: Reviews mention slowness at a specific stage (ordering, paying, food coming out).
  - id="wait-time-online-queue" kind=remediation themes=[wait_time] verticals=universal
    title: Add an online queue or callback system
    body: If wait-time complaints come from Reviewers physically queueing on-site, an online queue (SMS callback, virtual ticket) typically cuts the perceived wait by half within two weeks.
    signals: Wait-time complaints describe standing in line, not in-seat waits.
  - id="staff-attitude-policy-clarity" kind=remediation themes=[staff_attitude,service] verticals=universal
    title: Clarify the policy the team was enforcing
    body: Many attitude complaints stem from a staff member enforcing a real policy (no refunds after X, no substitutions) without the soft skills to explain it warmly. Write the policy + the warm-explanation script together.
    signals: Reviewer is angry about a refusal or refusal-style interaction.
  - id="restaurant-food-safety-audit" kind=remediation themes=[cleanliness,product_quality] verticals=restaurant,cafe
    title: Run an internal food-safety audit before the next inspection
    body: A Reviewer mentioning anything food-safety adjacent (illness, off taste, hair, cross-contamination) should trigger a same-week internal audit. Document temperatures, dates, and handwashing compliance.
    signals: Any Review hints at illness, off taste, or cross-contamination.
  - id="restaurant-table-turn-review" kind=remediation themes=[wait_time] verticals=restaurant,cafe
    title: Recalculate table-turn assumptions against actual data
    body: Pull the last four Friday/Saturday seatings and measure actual table-turn vs. the planned cadence. Most weekend wait-time complaints come from a 5–10 minute overrun no one is tracking.
    signals: Wait-time complaints concentrate on weekend evenings.
  - id="reinforce-named-staff-shoutout" kind=reinforcement themes=[service,staff_attitude] verticals=universal
    title: Shout out the named staff member in the team channel
    body: Reviewers naming a specific team member positively is the highest-signal compliment you'll get. Share the quote (with the Reviewer anonymised if needed) in the team channel and reference it in the next 1-on-1.
    signals: Positive Review names a specific staff member.
  - id="reinforce-replicate-the-good-shift" kind=reinforcement themes=[service,wait_time,cleanliness] verticals=universal
    title: Identify what was different about the praised shift
    body: Find the shift that prompted the positive Reviews and list what was different: who was on, what was prepped, what wasn't. Replicating a good shift is more reliable than trying to fix a bad one.
    signals: Cluster of positive Reviews on a specific day / shift.
  - id="reinforce-share-on-storefront" kind=reinforcement themes=[service,product_quality,cleanliness,staff_attitude] verticals=universal
    title: Pin the strongest Review of the week to your storefront
    body: A single specific, recent positive Review on your Google / Yelp profile is worth more than ten generic five-stars. Pin the strongest one and link to it from your website.
    signals: At least one detailed positive Review with a quotable line.
  - id="reinforce-loyalty-thanks" kind=reinforcement themes=[pricing,service] verticals=universal
    title: Send a personal thanks to repeat positive Reviewers
    body: Reviewers who post a second positive Review months apart are your most valuable advocates. A brief personal thanks (not a discount) signals you noticed and tends to produce a third Review.
    signals: Same Reviewer has posted positively before.
  - id="reinforce-document-the-good" kind=reinforcement themes=[product_quality,cleanliness,accessibility,other] verticals=universal
    title: Document what's working so the team doesn't drift
    body: Whatever earned the positive Reviews this week is at risk of quiet erosion. Write a one-paragraph note in the operations log describing the practice, the team, and the period — your future self will thank you.
    signals: Two or more positive Reviews praising the same operational detail.
  - id="reinforce-ask-for-photo" kind=reinforcement themes=[product_quality] verticals=universal
    title: Invite the happy Reviewer to add a photo
    body: Positive Reviews with photos sit higher in Source rankings and convert better. Reply to the Reviewer thanking them and gently invite a photo if they took one.
    signals: Detailed positive Review with no photo attached.
  - id="reinforce-loop-back-after-complaint-resolved" kind=reinforcement themes=[service,other] verticals=universal
    title: Loop back to Reviewers whose past complaint you fixed
    body: If a previous-month complaint has been addressed, message that Reviewer with a one-line update. The strongest second Reviews come from Reviewers who saw their feedback taken seriously.
    signals: A previously-complaining Reviewer is silent this week and the underlying issue is fixed.

Review quotes (redacted, draw evidence verbatim from this list):
  - reviewId="r-wait-2" stars=3 themes=[wait_time,service]
    quote: Long queue at the till around midday — fewer staff than usual.
  - reviewId="r-service-1" stars=1 themes=[service]
    quote: [REVIEWER] was ignored at the counter for ten minutes.
  - reviewId="r-mixed-1" stars=4 themes=[service]
    quote: Mostly good, slight slow service.
  - reviewId="r-wait-1" stars=2 themes=[wait_time]
    quote: Waited 25 minutes for a sandwich at lunch on Friday.
  - reviewId="r-quality-1" stars=2 themes=[product_quality]
    quote: The pastries were stale on Tuesday morning.

Produce the <output>...</output> JSON per the system prompt.

assertions.description: every patternId is present in PLAYBOOK
every evidence.redactedQuote appears verbatim in the input reviews

topPatterns.title: Shout out the named staff member in the team channel
topPatterns.tailoredBody: Two Reviewers this week named a specific staff member positively — share the quotes (anonymising the Reviewer) in the team channel and reference them at the next 1-on-1.

evidence.redactedQuote: The team are wonderful — [REVIEWER] always remembers my order.

topPatterns.title: Pin the strongest Review of the week to your storefront
topPatterns.tailoredBody: You have a detailed 5-star Review praising the pastries and the morning team. Pin it to your Google profile and link to it from your website — a recent specific positive Review is worth more than ten generic five-stars.

evidence.redactedQuote: Best bakery in town. Pastries are perfect and the morning team is brilliant.

topPatterns.title: Identify what was different about the praised shift
topPatterns.tailoredBody: The positive Reviews this week cluster on Saturday morning shifts. List who was on, what was prepped, what was different — replicating a good shift is more reliable than fixing a bad one.

evidence.redactedQuote: Saturday morning visit was perfect — quick service and great coffee.

assertions.description: every patternId belongs to a Pattern with kind="reinforcement"

topPatterns.title: Audit restroom cleaning frequency against peak traffic
topPatterns.tailoredBody: Two Reviews this week specifically named the restrooms as the issue, both on Friday/Saturday evenings. Double your cleaning intervals during those peak hours and photograph before/after for the team channel.

evidence.redactedQuote: Restrooms were filthy on Saturday evening — clearly hadn't been cleaned in hours.

topPatterns.title: Coach the named team member privately within 48 hours
topPatterns.tailoredBody: A Reviewer named a specific bartender as being dismissive. Have a private, non-disciplinary coaching conversation in the next 48 hours — don't share the Review verbatim; paraphrase the behaviour.

evidence.redactedQuote: The bartender was rude and dismissive when I asked for a substitution.

topPatterns.title: Run a service-recovery outreach to the week's unhappy Reviewers
topPatterns.tailoredBody: Three of the five Reviews this week were 1-2 stars naming service breakdowns. A personal apology + remediation offer within 24 hours halves the chance of a follow-up complaint reaching social media.

evidence.redactedQuote: Worst service experience of my year — nobody acknowledged us.

  - id="accessibility-staff-training" kind=remediation themes=[accessibility,staff_attitude] verticals=universal
    title: Brief the team on accessible-service basics
    body: Run a 15-minute team brief covering the basics: ask before helping, speak directly to the customer (not their companion), keep aisles clear. Reviewers more often complain about the interaction than the building itself.
    signals: Accessibility complaint describes a staff interaction, not a physical barrier.
  - id="restaurant-food-safety-audit" kind=remediation themes=[cleanliness,product_quality] verticals=restaurant,cafe
    title: Run an internal food-safety audit before the next inspection
    body: A Reviewer mentioning anything food-safety adjacent (illness, off taste, hair, cross-contamination) should trigger a same-week internal audit. Document temperatures, dates, and handwashing compliance.
    signals: Any Review hints at illness, off taste, or cross-contamination.
  - id="reinforce-named-staff-shoutout" kind=reinforcement themes=[service,staff_attitude] verticals=universal
    title: Shout out the named staff member in the team channel
    body: Reviewers naming a specific team member positively is the highest-signal compliment you'll get. Share the quote (with the Reviewer anonymised if needed) in the team channel and reference it in the next 1-on-1.
    signals: Positive Review names a specific staff member.
  - id="reinforce-replicate-the-good-shift" kind=reinforcement themes=[service,wait_time,cleanliness] verticals=universal
    title: Identify what was different about the praised shift
    body: Find the shift that prompted the positive Reviews and list what was different: who was on, what was prepped, what wasn't. Replicating a good shift is more reliable than trying to fix a bad one.
    signals: Cluster of positive Reviews on a specific day / shift.
  - id="reinforce-share-on-storefront" kind=reinforcement themes=[service,product_quality,cleanliness,staff_attitude] verticals=universal
    title: Pin the strongest Review of the week to your storefront
    body: A single specific, recent positive Review on your Google / Yelp profile is worth more than ten generic five-stars. Pin the strongest one and link to it from your website.
    signals: At least one detailed positive Review with a quotable line.
  - id="reinforce-loyalty-thanks" kind=reinforcement themes=[pricing,service] verticals=universal
    title: Send a personal thanks to repeat positive Reviewers
    body: Reviewers who post a second positive Review months apart are your most valuable advocates. A brief personal thanks (not a discount) signals you noticed and tends to produce a third Review.
    signals: Same Reviewer has posted positively before.
  - id="reinforce-document-the-good" kind=reinforcement themes=[product_quality,cleanliness,accessibility,other] verticals=universal
    title: Document what's working so the team doesn't drift
    body: Whatever earned the positive Reviews this week is at risk of quiet erosion. Write a one-paragraph note in the operations log describing the practice, the team, and the period — your future self will thank you.
    signals: Two or more positive Reviews praising the same operational detail.
  - id="reinforce-loop-back-after-complaint-resolved" kind=reinforcement themes=[service,other] verticals=universal
    title: Loop back to Reviewers whose past complaint you fixed
    body: If a previous-month complaint has been addressed, message that Reviewer with a one-line update. The strongest second Reviews come from Reviewers who saw their feedback taken seriously.
    signals: A previously-complaining Reviewer is silent this week and the underlying issue is fixed.

Review quotes (redacted, draw evidence verbatim from this list):
  - reviewId="r-neg-1" stars=1 themes=[cleanliness]
    quote: Restrooms were filthy on Saturday evening — clearly hadn't been cleaned in hours.
  - reviewId="r-neg-4" stars=2 themes=[cleanliness]
    quote: Filthy floors throughout.
  - reviewId="r-neg-3" stars=1 themes=[service]
    quote: Worst service experience of my year — nobody acknowledged us.
  - reviewId="r-neg-2" stars=1 themes=[staff_attitude]
    quote: The bartender was rude and dismissive when I asked for a substitution.

Produce the <output>...</output> JSON per the system prompt.

handler skips a zero-Review period without calling composeDigest, inserting a row, or sending mail

topPatterns.title: Switch walk-ins to a virtual queue for the next two Saturdays
topPatterns.tailoredBody: Two Reviewers complained about walk-in queues blocking the shop on Saturday. A free virtual-queue app for two Saturdays will tell you whether the underlying issue is throughput or perception.

evidence.redactedQuote: Walk-in queue was out the door on Saturday morning.

topPatterns.title: Review peak-hour staffing levels against last quarter's rota
topPatterns.tailoredBody: Three wait-time mentions this week — pull the last quarter's Saturday rota and compare staffing on the floor against what it used to be on equivalent peak Saturdays.

evidence.redactedQuote: Booked appointments running 20 minutes late even though I arrived on time.

topPatterns.title: Run a service-recovery outreach to the week's unhappy Reviewers
topPatterns.tailoredBody: You have two 1-2 star Reviews this week from booked customers who felt their slot was deprioritised for walk-ins. A short personal apology in the next 24 hours typically halves the risk of escalation.

evidence.redactedQuote: Walk-in queue was out the door on Saturday morning.

results.capturedUserMessage:
Business: Acme Cuts
Business industry: barbershop

Theme aggregates (current week vs previous week):
  - service: current=2, previous=1
  - wait_time: current=3, previous=1

Candidate Patterns (you must pick 3 from this list):
  - id="service-recovery-outreach" kind=remediation themes=[service] verticals=universal
    title: Run a service-recovery outreach to the week's unhappy Reviewers
    body: Identify the Reviewers who left 1–2 star Reviews mentioning service breakdowns this week and draft a personal apology + remediation offer. A 24-hour response window typically halves the chance of a follow-up complaint reaching social media.
    signals: Multiple service-themed 1–2 star Reviews in the same week.
  - id="service-script-refresh" kind=remediation themes=[service] verticals=universal
    title: Refresh front-line scripts for the top recurring complaint
    body: Pull the single most repeated service complaint from this week's Reviews and write a 3-line response script the team can use when a customer raises it. Practise it at the next shift huddle.
    signals: Same service complaint repeats across 3+ Reviews.
  - id="service-shadow-shift" kind=remediation themes=[service] verticals=universal
    title: Shadow a full shift to confirm the gap is real
    body: Spend one peak shift observing the customer journey end-to-end without intervening. Most repeating service complaints surface within 30 minutes of watching the live flow.
    signals: Vague service complaints that don't point at one root cause.
  - id="service-feedback-loop" kind=remediation themes=[service,other] verticals=universal
    title: Close the loop with the Reviewer once the fix lands
    body: When a service issue raised in a Review is fixed, message the Reviewer with a one-line update. Showing you read and acted on the Review is the fastest path to a revised rating.
    signals: Reviewer mentions a specific fixable issue (not a one-off mood complaint).
  - id="product-quality-secret-shopper" kind=remediation themes=[product_quality,service] verticals=universal
    title: Run a secret-shopper pass on the flagged item
    body: Have someone unfamiliar to the team order or use the item and report back with photos. Often catches the quality drift the regular team has stopped noticing.
    signals: Repeated complaints about an item the team insists is fine.
  - id="wait-time-staffing-review" kind=remediation themes=[wait_time] verticals=universal
    title: Review peak-hour staffing levels against last quarter's rota
    body: Compare the rota for the days Reviewers complained about waiting with the same days last quarter. Most wait-time clusters trace back to a single under-staffed shift pattern.
    signals: wait_time mentioned 3+ times in a single week.
  - id="wait-time-queue-comms" kind=remediation themes=[wait_time] verticals=universal
    title: Set an explicit wait-time expectation at the door
    body: Reviewers tolerate waits twice as long when the expected duration is stated up front. Brief the host / greeter to quote a current wait and offer a wait-list SMS.
    signals: Reviewer says the wait was unexpected, not that it was long.
  - id="wait-time-throughput-bottleneck" kind=remediation themes=[wait_time] verticals=universal
    title: Time the bottleneck step end-to-end on a busy shift
    body: Pick the step Reviewers complain about (order-taking, prep, payment) and stopwatch it across 20 customers on a busy shift. Throughput problems almost always live in one step, not the whole flow.
    signals: Reviews mention slowness at a specific stage (ordering, paying, food coming out).
  - id="wait-time-online-queue" kind=remediation themes=[wait_time] verticals=universal
    title: Add an online queue or callback system
    body: If wait-time complaints come from Reviewers physically queueing on-site, an online queue (SMS callback, virtual ticket) typically cuts the perceived wait by half within two weeks.
    signals: Wait-time complaints describe standing in line, not in-seat waits.
  - id="staff-attitude-policy-clarity" kind=remediation themes=[staff_attitude,service] verticals=universal
    title: Clarify the policy the team was enforcing
    body: Many attitude complaints stem from a staff member enforcing a real policy (no refunds after X, no substitutions) without the soft skills to explain it warmly. Write the policy + the warm-explanation script together.
    signals: Reviewer is angry about a refusal or refusal-style interaction.
  - id="barbershop-walkin-management" kind=remediation themes=[wait_time,service] verticals=barbershop,salon
    title: Switch walk-ins to a virtual queue for the next two Saturdays
    body: Walk-in queues physically clog the shop and frustrate booked customers. A free virtual-queue app for two Saturdays will tell you whether the underlying issue is throughput or perception.
    signals: Wait-time or service complaints on weekends with mention of walk-ins.
  - id="salon-consultation-discipline" kind=remediation themes=[service,product_quality] verticals=salon,barbershop
    title: Reinforce the pre-service consultation script
    body: Most service / outcome complaints in personal-care trace back to a rushed consultation. Require a 3-minute consultation with reference photos for every colour / cut service this week.
    signals: Reviewer says the outcome didn't match what they asked for.
  - id="reinforce-named-staff-shoutout" kind=reinforcement themes=[service,staff_attitude] verticals=universal
    title: Shout out the named staff member in the team channel
    body: Reviewers naming a specific team member positively is the highest-signal compliment you'll get. Share the quote (with the Reviewer anonymised if needed) in the team channel and reference it in the next 1-on-1.
    signals: Positive Review names a specific staff member.
  - id="reinforce-replicate-the-good-shift" kind=reinforcement themes=[service,wait_time,cleanliness] verticals=universal
    title: Identify what was different about the praised shift
    body: Find the shift that prompted the positive Reviews and list what was different: who was on, what was prepped, what wasn't. Replicating a good shift is more reliable than trying to fix a bad one.
    signals: Cluster of positive Reviews on a specific day / shift.
  - id="reinforce-share-on-storefront" kind=reinforcement themes=[service,product_quality,cleanliness,staff_attitude] verticals=universal
    title: Pin the strongest Review of the week to your storefront
    body: A single specific, recent positive Review on your Google / Yelp profile is worth more than ten generic five-stars. Pin the strongest one and link to it from your website.
    signals: At least one detailed positive Review with a quotable line.
  - id="reinforce-loyalty-thanks" kind=reinforcement themes=[pricing,service] verticals=universal
    title: Send a personal thanks to repeat positive Reviewers
    body: Reviewers who post a second positive Review months apart are your most valuable advocates. A brief personal thanks (not a discount) signals you noticed and tends to produce a third Review.
    signals: Same Reviewer has posted positively before.
  - id="reinforce-loop-back-after-complaint-resolved" kind=reinforcement themes=[service,other] verticals=universal
    title: Loop back to Reviewers whose past complaint you fixed
    body: If a previous-month complaint has been addressed, message that Reviewer with a one-line update. The strongest second Reviews come from Reviewers who saw their feedback taken seriously.
    signals: A previously-complaining Reviewer is silent this week and the underlying issue is fixed.

Review quotes (redacted, draw evidence verbatim from this list):
  - reviewId="r-bs-1" stars=2 themes=[wait_time,service]
    quote: Walk-in queue was out the door on Saturday morning.
  - reviewId="r-bs-2" stars=3 themes=[wait_time]
    quote: Booked appointments running 20 minutes late even though I arrived on time.
